ops.body: Landed as cc-squash bcd22961 (feat(proxy): on-path inline-lossless fast-lane), CI green. Hardened through FOUR adversarial review rounds (codex gpt-5.6-sol xhigh refuters, fable synthesis): r1 found the Hold-precedence hole + L1-blindness cluster; r2 fixed precedence (committed keys always render; staged wins only when it splices), added un-commit-on-staged-application, monotonic floor, closed-at-birth window; r3 re-derived un-commit from base∩commitment (pinned-transition edge) + kill-switch window close; r4 replaced the MAX sentinel with explicit window_closed bool + monotonic floor (race-safe both interleavings) + shadow-forward close. Final verdict: all prompt-correctness invariants PASS; residual concurrent-session one-bust exposures are pre-existing in kind and tracked (f0748e2 hardening, 24d181d pricing, ad0664f restart). LIVE VERIFY (2026-07-14, release build, 3 sessions through the real proxy): engine live-proven — L1 staged plan ×3, refs.db 3 rows, all requests forwarded clean (no parse warns, breaker quiet, sessions completed); the fast-lane line itself did NOT fire, structurally explained: sequential tool cadence leaves only the pinned current pair past the floor, and parallel multi-result messages are excluded by the sole_squash_target guard — the lane's firing shape is a tool pair with a trailing message in the SAME request (interactive CC's injected system-reminders), which -p mode cannot produce. Firing semantics are pinned by 11 unit/integration tests. Criterion b5587f2 (live log line) therefore needs one interactive tool-heavy session: ./bin/ccs run -- --model opus, then grep 'L2 fast-lane' ~/.cc-squash/daemon.log.
